title: UN resolution urges reparations for slavery’s ‘historical wrongs’
content: Applause erupted in the UN General Assembly Hall on Wednesday as Member States adopted a resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: UN chief calls for immediate release of 118 detained UN staff
content: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has called for the immediate release of the 118 UN staff currently detained worldwide as attacks against the United Nations increase with 179 personnel arrested or detained last year alone.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: UN calls for solidarity and political will to stamp out racial discrimination
content: Nearly 70 years after South African police fired upon peaceful demonstrators in Sharpeville protesting apartheid-era laws, killing 69, the UN renewed the commitment to work for justice and equality on Monday, marking the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis
content: Since the start of the Middle East conflict with Israeli and US strikes on Iran on 28 February, concerns have been growing over rising oil and commodity prices.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations
content: Born from the ashes of the Second World War, the dream of a more peaceful and fairer world drew nations together in 1945 under a new vision for humanity: the United Nations.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: When justice fails: Why women can’t get protection from AI deepfake abuse
content: She woke up to messages flooding her phone. Doctored images of her, sexualised and viral, had spread while she slept.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: A former refugee takes on a world in flight
content: Barham Salih has spent much of his life crossing borders, first as a young Kurdish exile fleeing repression in Iraq, and now as the UN top official for refugees, confronting a world in which more than 117 million people have been forced from their homes.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: Nearly 5 million children are still dying annually before their fifth birthday: Here’s why
content: An estimated 4.9 million children died before their fifth birthday in 2024, including 2.3 million newborns, according to new United Nations estimates released on Tuesday – highlighting a worrying slowdown in global progress on child survival.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Over a billion fear losing land and homes within five years
content: Despite global progress in strengthening land tenure and governance, more than a billion people worldwide – nearly one in four adults – fear they could lose the rights to some or all of their land and housing within the next five years.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Science-led governance of AI can help power sustainable development: Guterres
content: UN Secretary-General António Guterres highlighted the key role science has in international governance of artificial intelligence during an event on Friday held on the margins of the AI Impact Summit in New Delhi, India.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: From India, Guterres calls for $3 billion fund to ensure AI benefits all
content: The future of artificial intelligence “cannot be decided by a handful of countries or left to the whims of a few billionaires,” the UN Secretary-General told the AI Impact Summit in New Delhi on Thursday, calling for a global fund to help developing nations to better access these technologies.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Decades of gains overshadowed as Asia-Pacific falls behind on sustainable development targets
content: Climate inaction, biodiversity loss and rising emissions are pushing Asia and the Pacific further off course on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), with the region set to miss nearly nine out of ten targets by 2030, the United Nations has warned.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Floods, drought and insecurity drive acute hunger for 7.5 million in Pakistan
content: After a year that saw heavy monsoon floods, prolonged drought and dry spells, and a surge in violence, 7.5 million people in Pakistan face high levels of food insecurity and malnutrition according to a report from the global hunger monitor.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: UN forum calls for overhaul of agrifood systems
content: Rising food prices and declining farm incomes are putting increasing pressure on the global food system, with up to 720 million people facing hunger last year, and billions more struggling to afford healthy diets.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: UN social policy commission closes with calls to turn pledges into action
content: With inequality deepening and trust in public institutions under strain, the UN’s main forum on social policy wrapped up its annual session on Tuesday with a renewed push to turn global commitments on social justice into action.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Welcome to the ‘agrihood’ – the neighbourhood of the future?
content: Towns and cities are home to more than half of the world’s population and responsible for around 70 per cent of the greenhouse gas emissions driving the climate crisis, which is why urban planners in Brazil are leading a design revolution that could point the way to creating built-up areas with a dramatically smaller carbon footprint.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Choose peace over chaos, Guterres urges as he sets out final-year priorities
content: With global tensions rising and “reckless actions” triggering dangerous consequences, UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Thursday called for renewed efforts on peace, justice and sustainable development as he outlined his priorities for 2026 – the final year of his tenure.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: From family farm to climate tech: How one Kenyan woman is helping farmers outsmart drought
content: “Giving up is not an option - so many people depend on you,” the words of Maryanne Gichanga, a participant in a UN supported initiative, which aims to help farmers in Kenya find solutions to alleviate the pressures of climate change on agricultural production.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Eighty years at the heart of global development
content: From humanitarian crises and youth unemployment, to climate resilience and development financing, many of today’s global challenges pass through a single United Nations body that is quietly turning 80 this year.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: UN chief warns of a world in chaos as impunity and unpredictability spreads
content: In his final annual address outlining his priorities, UN Secretary-General António Guterres warned that the world is “brimming with conflict, impunity, inequality and unpredictability” – even as international cooperation is fraying at the moment it is most needed.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: ‘Beyond GDP’ economists push for clearer metrics on wellbeing, sustainability
content: Top finance experts are meeting at UN Geneva this week to push for a radical shake up in the way economic growth is gauged, in response to concerns that GDP measurements provide little insight about progress on key sustainability targets that are vital to our survival.

sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: Iraq ‘unrecognisable and remarkable’ after years of conflict: UN coordinator
content: Some two decades after a shaky political transition, Iraq today is a country “at peace, with increased security and a clear determination to win the battle of development,” says the UN resident coordinator for Iraq on Wednesday.

title: Development cooperation agreement marks new phase in UN-Iraq partnership

title: First Person: Felipe Paullier, the youngest ever senior UN official, charged with giving a voice to young people around the world
content: A paediatrician by training, Felipe Paullier has long been an advocate for youth. Formerly Director of the Uruguayan Government's Youth Institute, a role which involved several joint actions with various UN agencies, he was appointed to run the UN Youth Office in 2023, the youngest senior appointment in United Nations history.

sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | SDGs

title: UN sounds alarm over rising demands on water resources as scarcity increases

title: UN and partners back new measures to help millions move from vulnerability to opportunity

title: From milking stalls to mobile apps: UN-supported project powers rural revival in Indonesia

title: UNICEF: 20% of world’s children still trapped in extreme poverty

title: New Brazil-led fund aims to put forest protection at the heart of climate action

title: Doha summit ends with call to turn social pledges into action

title: Progress in protecting women is real but fragile, UN-backed report warns

title: LIVE coverage from Doha: Second World Summit for Social Development

title: UN mourns constitutional lawyer-turned top crisis diplomat, Nicholas Haysom

title: At UN, Malala, Anne Hathaway call for action on women’s rights

title: UN commission opens with focus on justice for women and girls
content: Access to justice for women and girls dominated the agenda on Monday as the Commission on the Status of Women opened its 70th session at UN Headquarters in New York. Government officials, civil society representatives and UN leaders called for renewed efforts to dismantle the discrimination and legal obstacles that continue to limit women’s and girls’ rights worldwide.

title: New challenges bring increased risks in combating child sexual abuse, exploitation

title: LIVE: International Women’s Day and CSW70 at the United Nations
content: Welcome to our live coverage of International Women’s Day 2026 and the opening of the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women at UN Headquarters in New York. Justice. Action” for all women and girls. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.

title: Women’s representation in parliament sees sluggish gains
content: Women held 27.5 per cent of parliamentary seats worldwide as the year began –a 0.3 per cent increase from 2025, marking the slowest growth in nearly a decade, according to a new report from the UN-backed Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU).

title: UN calls for fair play in the global race for critical minerals
content: Critical minerals such as lithium and cobalt are central to the transition to a zero-carbon economy. As the Security Council meets on Thursday to discuss “energy, critical minerals and security,” here is some of the work the UN is doing to ensure that the transition is just and equitable.
